_note: Per-lesson role-play scenario. Grounded in B1/005 dialogue + vocab. Learner plays Mia; computer plays Leo. Each NPC node offers 2-3 branches so the same lesson language can be reached different ways. Consumed by RP.Engine + RP.classifiers.local (see roleplay/).
title: 005 · A Difficult Decision

npc.text: I have a difficult decision to make.

routes.meaning: Mia asks what the problem is.
examples: What's the problem?
What is the problem?
Oh no, what's the problem?
What's wrong?

routes.meaning: Mia offers to help Leo decide.
examples: Maybe I can help you decide.
I can help you if you want.
Let me help you with it.
routes.hint: 💬 You can offer help, or ask “What's the problem?”

partial.message: Ask Leo what's wrong: “What's the problem?”

off_topic.message: Leo needs help deciding. Ask “What's the problem?”

npc.text: Thanks! Let me tell you the problem.

routes.meaning: Mia asks what the problem is.
examples: What's the problem?
Okay, what's the problem?
So what is it?

npc.text: I can join the music club or the soccer team, but not both.

routes.meaning: Mia asks which one Leo likes more.
examples: Which one do you like more?
Which do you like more?
Which one do you prefer?
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “Which one do you like more?”

routes.meaning: Mia asks why he can't do both.
examples: Why can't you do both?
Why not both?
Can't you join both?
routes.hint: 💬 You can ask “Why can't you do both?” or “Which one do you like more?”

partial.message: Ask about his preference: “Which one do you like more?”

off_topic.message: Leo has two choices. Ask “Which one do you like more?”

npc.text: They meet at the same time, so I can only pick one.

routes.meaning: Mia asks which one Leo likes more.
examples: Which one do you like more?
Okay, which one do you like more?
So which do you like more?
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “Which one do you like more?”

partial.message: Ask “Which one do you like more?”

off_topic.message: Ask Leo “Which one do you like more?”

npc.text: I love music, but soccer keeps me healthy.

routes.meaning: Mia advises Leo to choose what he enjoys most.
examples: I think you should choose the one you enjoy most.
You should choose the one you enjoy most.
I think you should pick the one you enjoy most.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “I think you should choose the one you enjoy most.”

routes.meaning: Mia points out that staying healthy matters.
examples: Staying healthy is important too.
But soccer keeps you healthy.
Health is important, though.
routes.hint: 💬 You can talk about health, or advise “I think you should choose the one you enjoy most.”

partial.message: Give advice: “I think you should choose the one you enjoy most.”

off_topic.message: Help Leo decide. Say “I think you should choose the one you enjoy most.”

npc.text: That's true, staying healthy is important too.

routes.meaning: Mia advises Leo to choose what he enjoys most.
examples: I think you should choose the one you enjoy most.
Still, you should choose the one you enjoy most.
You should choose the one you enjoy most.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “I think you should choose the one you enjoy most.”

partial.message: Say “I think you should choose the one you enjoy most.”

off_topic.message: Advise Leo “Choose the one you enjoy most.”

npc.text: Maybe I could try music this year and soccer next year.

routes.meaning: Mia says that sounds like a smart plan.
examples: That sounds like a smart plan.
That's a smart plan.
That sounds like a really smart plan.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “That sounds like a smart plan.”

routes.meaning: Mia checks whether he's sure about that plan.
examples: Are you sure that works?
Can you really do both in two years?
Will you have time for both?
routes.hint: 💬 You can ask “Are you sure?” or say “That sounds like a smart plan.”

partial.message: Praise his idea: “That sounds like a smart plan.”

off_topic.message: Leo has an idea. Say “That sounds like a smart plan.”

npc.text: Yes, that way I don't have to give up either one.

routes.meaning: Mia says that sounds like a smart plan.
examples: That sounds like a smart plan.
That's a smart plan.
Okay, that sounds like a smart plan.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “That sounds like a smart plan.”

partial.message: Say “That sounds like a smart plan.”

off_topic.message: Tell Leo “That sounds like a smart plan.”

npc.text: You're right. I will choose the music club first.

routes.meaning: Mia wishes Leo good luck with his choice.
examples: Good luck! I'm sure you made the right choice.
Good luck! I'm sure you made the right decision.
Good luck! That's the right choice.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “Good luck! I'm sure you made the right choice.”

routes.meaning: Mia encourages Leo about his decision.
examples: You'll be great at the guitar!
You'll have so much fun in the music club!
I'm proud of you for deciding.
routes.hint: 💬 You can cheer him on, or say “Good luck! I'm sure you made the right choice.”

partial.message: Wish him luck: “Good luck! I'm sure you made the right choice.”

off_topic.message: Leo decided! Say “Good luck! I'm sure you made the right choice.”

npc.text: Thank you, Mia! I feel much better now.
Thanks for helping me decide. You're a great friend!
